notes: Reproduced the reported package size increase by downloading official NuGet artifacts and comparing 3.119.1 vs 3.119.2. Win32 grew from 13,050 KB to 75,013 KB (+61,963 KB, +474.8%), Linux grew from 39,465 KB to 53,907 KB (+14,442 KB, +36.6%), and macOS remained unchanged at 6,933 KB. Latest stable is still 3.119.2, and locally packed main-source NativeAssets packages are still similarly large (Win32 75,061 KB, Linux 52,921 KB), so the behavior persists and is not yet resolved in current main packaging outputs. Cross-platform Docker verification could not run because the local Docker daemon was unavailable.

versionResults.notes: Baseline package sizes are lower (Win32 13,050 KB; Linux 39,465 KB; macOS 6,933 KB).
Reported larger package sizes confirmed: Win32 75,013 KB, Linux 53,907 KB, macOS unchanged.
NuGet index shows latest stable is 3.119.2; issue still present on latest stable.
Packed NativeAssets projects from main produced similarly large packages (Win32 75,061 KB; Linux 52,921 KB; macOS 6,924 KB).

reproductionSteps.description: Collect .NET and host environment details in /tmp/repro-3519 for reproducibility context.
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/repro-3519 && cd /tmp/repro-3519 && dotnet --info && dotnet workload list && docker --version
reproductionSteps.output:
.NET SDK:
 Version:           10.0.102
 Commit:            4452502459
 Workload version:  10.0.102
 MSBuild version:   18.0.7+445250245

Runtime Environment:
 OS Name:     Mac OS X
 OS Version:  26.2
 OS Platform: Darwin
 RID:         osx-arm64
 Base Path:   /usr/local/share/dotnet/sdk/10.0.102/

reproductionSteps.description: Download NativeAssets NuGet packages for 3.119.1 and 3.119.2 (plus latest preview) and compute package size deltas.
reproductionSteps.command: for pkg in skiasharp.nativeassets.win32 skiasharp.nativeassets.linux skiasharp.nativeassets.macos; do for ver in 3.119.1 3.119.2 3.119.3-preview.1.1; do curl -fsSL https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/$pkg/$ver/$pkg.$ver.nupkg -o $pkg.$ver.nupkg; done; done && python3 size-diff.py

reproductionSteps.description: Confirm latest stable package version from NuGet to determine whether latest stable still exhibits the issue.
reproductionSteps.command: python3 -c "import json; v=json.load(open('win32-index.json'))['versions']; print([x for x in v if 'preview' not in x and 'stable' not in x][-1])"

reproductionSteps.description: Pack NativeAssets projects from current main source and compare resulting nupkg sizes.
reproductionSteps.command: cd $HOME/SkiaSharp && dotnet pack binding/SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Win32/SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Win32.csproj -c Release -o /tmp/repro-3519-mainpkgs && dotnet pack binding/SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux/SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux.csproj -c Release -o /tmp/repro-3519-mainpkgs && dotnet pack binding/SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.macOS/SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.macOS.csproj -c Release -o /tmp/repro-3519-mainpkgs

reproductionSteps.description: Attempt cross-platform verification in Docker Linux x64.
reproductionSteps.command: docker run --rm --platform linux/amd64 python:3.12-alpine sh -lc 'echo test'
reproductionSteps.output:
docker: Cannot connect to the Docker daemon at unix://$HOME/.docker/run/docker.sock. Is the docker daemon running?

Run 'docker run --help' for more information

errorMessages.primaryError: NativeAssets package sizes increase sharply in 3.119.2 compared with 3.119.1 (especially Win32), while macOS remains unchanged.

actionability.reason: Package size regression is reproducible from official NuGet artifacts and remains present in latest stable and locally packed main-source NativeAssets outputs.

workarounds: Pin NativeAssets packages to 3.119.1 if package size growth is blocking deployment constraints.
If security hardening is required, evaluate publishing a size-optimized package variant with stripped symbols where applicable.

proposedResponse.summary: Confirmed large NativeAssets size increase in 3.119.2; persists in current main packaging outputs.
proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for reporting this — I could reproduce the package size increase by downloading and comparing the official NuGet artifacts.

**Measured package sizes (KB):**
| Package | 3.119.1 | 3.119.2 | Delta |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Win32 | 13,050 | 75,013 | +61,963 (+474.8%) |
| SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.Linux | 39,465 | 53,907 | +14,442 (+36.6%) |
| SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.macOS | 6,933 | 6,933 | 0 |

I also verified that the latest stable on NuGet is still **3.119.2**, and locally packing NativeAssets from current `main` still produces similarly large outputs (Win32 ~75,061 KB, Linux ~52,921 KB). So this behavior is still present in current packaging outputs.

If you need an immediate mitigation, pinning to **3.119.1** avoids the size increase while this is investigated further.

actions.description: Ensure labels reflect native packaging regression scope.
Post reproduction matrix and measured package sizes.
actions.comment: Reproduced by direct NuGet package comparison: Win32 and Linux NativeAssets sizes increased significantly from 3.119.1 to 3.119.2, while macOS stayed flat. Latest stable (3.119.2) still shows the increase, and current main packaging outputs are similarly large.

corrections.upstream: Triage confidence for type/bug was moderate (0.65) due to potential intentional hardening trade-offs.
corrections.corrected: Reproduction confirms the reported behavior factually (large package size increase) across tested versions regardless of editorial classification.
